justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with explicit math showing sequential p99 sum of 1150ms vs 500ms inbound timeout, (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — downstream calls continue after upstream abandons, wasting resources, (3) Outbound timeout (1000ms) > inbound timeout (500ms) relationship explicitly called out, (4) Multiple remediation options provided including parallelization, timeout budget adjustment, upstream SLA renegotiation, cancellation propagation, and circuit breakers, (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional timeout math breakdown with ASCII diagram showing the 2.3x budget overrun and worst-case 3000ms wasted work scenario
Identified that InventoryService p99 alone (800ms) exceeds the 500ms inbound timeout, meaning even parallelization requires upstream SLA renegotiation
Thorough failure mode table covering cascading scenarios like simultaneous downstream degradation
Correctly escalated the parallel refactor to MJ (architect) and partial enrichment strategy to Bird (domain) — shows team awareness
Identified cancellation propagation gap — orphaned requests not just described but remediation (context deadline cancellation) specified
Prioritized recommendations into must-haves, should-haves, and nice-to-haves with clear blocking/non-blocking distinction
Caught secondary issues beyond the core timeout problem: missing circuit breakers, coupled health checks, no distributed tracing, no per-downstream metrics
